review_reason: 9 sorries remain after 5 iterations. All require proof restructuring: current exfalso-based approach is mathematically invalid for reflexive M cases. Iteration-based witness search (Pattern C) is the only viable path forward.

artifacts.summary: Pattern C infrastructure added (8 helper theorems/definitions). 6 sorries at lines 505, 586, 612, 895, 1306, 1410 - all in exfalso branches where no contradiction exists.
3 sorries at lines 210, 269, 316 - depend on density_frame_condition_strict
Detailed Pattern C implementation plan with code sketches
Final iteration summary - confirms proof restructuring required

partial_progress.details: Iteration 5 (FINAL): Confirmed all 9 sorries require fundamental proof restructuring. Key findings: (1) All sorries use exfalso in mathematically consistent configurations - no contradiction derivable. (2) When M ~ V (mutual CanonicalR), this is CONSISTENT, not contradictory. (3) seriality_escape theorem is NOT directly provable - Lindenbaum extension doesn't guarantee fresh G-formula. (4) Pattern C iteration with fuel is the ONLY viable solution. Resolution requires replacing by_cases+exfalso pattern with iteration-based witness search.

sorry_analysis.lines_505_586_612: Case B1 (M' reflexive): goal is ¬CanonicalR M' V or False, but configuration is consistent
sorry_analysis.lines_895_1306_1410: Case A with M reflexive DERIVED from h_VM assumption - mutual accessibility is consistent, not contradictory

blocker.description: Current proof structure fundamentally incompatible with remaining goals. Proof uses exfalso in branches where mutual accessibility (M ~ V) is mathematically consistent. No formula contradiction exists to derive False.
blocker.recommendation: Restructure density_frame_condition_strict to use iteration: (1) Remove nested by_cases + intro h_VM + exfalso pattern, (2) Instead, check if witness is strict via checkStrictness, (3) If not strict, apply density recursively with different distinguishing formula, (4) Prove termination via Nat.strongRecOn on subformula count

completion_data.completion_summary: Iterations 1-5 added Pattern C infrastructure (8 helper theorems/definitions) and reduced sorries from 13 to 9. Build passes. Final 9 sorries CANNOT be eliminated with current proof structure - all require proof restructuring from exfalso-based contradiction to iteration-based witness search. This is a fundamental mathematical limitation, not an implementation gap.
